posts.content: Thank you @elonmusk for saving America.
What the fuck do you think Elon Musk is going to do with your social security number??? Tweet it???? Open a line of credit you don’t have??????

July 2020

I had a user on here say they were going find where I live, murder me with their bare hands, and r*pe my dead corpse after setting it on fire.

Because I had a platform, internet sleuths found his identity and I was able to make a police report. 

This doesn’t happen 99% of the time. In fact, this was one of the ONLY times I had enough information about someone’s identity to make a police report during a decade on this platform. Most of the time my only available recourse is to block these people so they can’t comment on my things or DM me. 

I understand people *can* create second account but they hardly do that. And the reality for women is that many things that threaten our safety and well-being are not criminal offenses because the law has not caught up with the internet/tech.

I believe @elonmusk has an incredible vision for humanity and cannot express enough how thankful I am that he’s transforming this incredible platform but please listen to your users here. Removing the block feature is not the right move while anonymity is also protected. 

Maybe X should assign AI learning models to global user blocks and see what type of language and behavior users are personally blocking from their pages. I think there would be tremendous insight there.
Elon Musk is the greatest war general in modern history
@elonmusk Time to save America 🔥
Elon Musk is a hero
@elonmusk A modern tea party! https://t.co/f6WDYrgVwk
@elonmusk One of my favorites https://t.co/N28tGj6427

I have yet to read a substantive argument against the audit & defunding of USAID

Mostly just Democrat NPCs yelling about starving children that aren’t even being helped & the standard “Trump and Elon bad”

There has been a deliberate lack of transparency around USAID spending. We're talking about billions that go into essentially a deep state slush fund, with little to ZERO oversight on where the money actually ends up.

They act like money is being taken from dying kids at St. Jude’s when in reality it’s being taken from gay performance arts in Uganda, Big Bird in Baghdad, and useless NGOs where execs are paid $400k+ a year to advance DEI in Serbia.  

The American people deserve to know that they have been robbed to fuel bureaucratic inefficiencies, Democrat coffers, and CIA-backed political agendas.

Congrats to the brilliant @VivekGRamaswamy on his appointment to lead the Department of Government Efficiency with Elon.

Vivek is the best mind to help dismantle the Bureaucratic State which has had a chokehold on America for far too long.

Godspeed my friend! And AFUERA! 🔥 https://t.co/psxhLA6fU1

Had Trump stayed in office, Elon wouldn't have gone full-blown anti-Soros. Having companies of immense national interest screwed by the Biden-Harris Admin simply because Elon stuck his head out of the Overton window was a pivotal point that may have saved America.
@BridgetPhetasy @elonmusk May have saved America

Trump announced on the campaign trail he was going to employ Elon to execute DOGE and do exactly what is being done right now. The people absolutely voted for this. That’s that democracy you guys love so much!!!

The Governor of Pennsylvania is threatening to arrest Elon Musk

They are absolutely terrified to lose Pennsylvania.

@krishnanrohit U could give any one of those critics $10 billion dollars and say “go build something great, and hire whoever you need” and absolutely none of them would be able to do a fraction of what Elon has done with SpaceX or Tesla.

It’s insane to watch Democrats demand The Washington Post make an endorsement of Kamala and have a meltdown over neutrality in Media

I truly believe The Washington Post refusing to endorse a presidential candidate never would have happened without Elon Musk

Merely having the bravery to speak out about his political views, which are polar opposite of what was socially acceptable in Silicon Valley and Hollywood, was absolutely contagious.

It showed others that not only can you be outspoken about your beliefs, but you can also just choose to not be a mouthpiece for the Democrat party as you had to be in the past.

A core pillar of honest journalism is objectivity, yet Democrats are demanding hyper-partisan activism or else. This is not what journalism is supposed to be but has unfortunately become. Kudos to The Washington Post— I hope more outlets move towards objectivity, and we can Make Journalism Great Again!

This recent New York Times article, “Elon Musk Has Crossed The Line” unintentionally makes the case for Musk’s position on the ADL & here’s why:

The New York Times paints Musk as attacking the ADL for no reason

They state that the ADL’s CEO Jonathan Greenblatt had previously praised Musk in October 2022 by stating Musk is “The Henry Ford of our time.” (They conveniently omit that just a month later, Greenblatt called for an advertiser boycott of X because of Musk’s reinstatement of Donald Trump, but I digress)

The New York Times then starts RIPPING ON JONATHAN GREENBLATT for a “tone-deaf” praise of Henry Ford because Henry Ford was anti-Semitic. 

THIS is precisely the battle between Musk and the ADL. The boundaries of acceptable, non-offensive speech are so impossible to keep up with that even the ADL can’t keep up and is being scolded by The New York Times. 

The difference is that the ADL is demanding that Musk and X censor individuals & adhere to speech standards that the censors themselves cannot adhere to and whose boundaries are continuously shifting. This is the issue with censoring “hate speech” in general. 

“Hate speech” does not exist and is totally subjective— what is hate speech to me may not be hate speech to you. Entertaining the idea of banning “hate speech” allows whoever is in power to take away one of the core principles of a free society based on completely subjective boundaries. 

We must allow even the most reprehensible speech, from those we most vehemently disagree with, to prevent the sword from eventually being turned towards us. 

“First they came for the Communists
And I did not speak out
Because I was not a Communist
Then they came for the Socialists
And I did not speak out
Because I was not a Socialist
Then they came for the trade unionists
And I did not speak out
Because I was not a trade unionist
Then they came for the Jews
And I did not speak out
Because I was not a Jew
Then they came for me
And there was no one left
To speak out for me.”

The purchase of Twitter / 𝕏 was the single greatest thing to happen for free speech on internet. We should keep that way, and that requires maximum transparency about what is happening to many accounts. 

This isn’t a new call either. We have been saying this for a while.

Nobody is “pretending”

Neither of us have a “deal with Elon” but reading this thread it sounds like you have some conspiracy that we just hype this platform to be boosted/monetized which is false. 

In fact, I make significantly less than many accounts smaller than me who are monetized.

Alx and I have both been on and have cared about this platform for a long time and are genuinely stoked to see it evolving in a direction that many perceive to be positive. 

It’s my position that the platform is moving in a great direction in terms of free speech, but I also recognize that completely gutting this platform to undo a decade of coded censorship is no easy task and will take time. 

If you cannot recognize how much freer the dialogue on this platform has been, and how quickly many Mainstream Media narratives have been dismantled through real time information on 𝕏 in a way that was not possible a year ago, I don’t know what to tell you.

If you don’t agree and think things need to move quicker and that nothing matters until Alex Jones is free, there’s tons of X employees you can go air those grievances to. However, I would suggest articulating it in a way other than whining under a post and accusing people of stupid conspiracies.

.@elonmusk: “You can think of [excessive regulations and laws] like Gulliver’s Travels…

If the nation is Gulliver, it’s being tied down by one little regulatory string at a time. Eventually you have millions of strings and then the giant can’t move.

So I think there needs to be something where we delete rules, regulations, and laws. If all we do is add them, eventually we will be able to do nothing”

Yeah Elon waterboarded us in Twitter HQ until we posted.

I’m actually the one who started the train of people posting payout numbers. My original tweet emphasized being transparent to encourage other creators to post on Twitter. What followed was entirely organic. 

This is an incredible moment for Twitter, free speech, and empowering creators which you should be happy about. 

You are once again creating drama out of literally nothing.

Really incredible that people accuse me of saying nice things about this platform because of ad revenue

I’ve been here for a long time, posting for free. I loved Twitter & always preferred it to Instagram + Facebook

I just think most people nowadays don’t have any patience & want things to be perfect/fixed instantaneously.
